CREATE TABLE test_table (id integer NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, char_code integer NOT NULL);
SELECT id, char_code, chr(char_code) AS character FROM test_table;

CREATE PROCEDURE test_chr_boundary AS BEGIN DECLARE str varchar; str := 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'; DECLARE pos integer; pos := 0; DECLARE c_code integer; DECLARE c_char varchar; WHILE (pos < length(str)) DO result(aref(str, pos), ascii(subseq(str, pos, pos+1, 1)), chr(aref(str, pos))); pos := pos + 1; END; END;

SELECT t.id, t.char_code, chr(t.char_code) AS character, (SELECT MAX(char_code) FROM test_table) AS max_code FROM test_table t WHERE t.char_code < (SELECT MIN(char_code) FROM test_table WHERE id <> 1);
CREATE TABLE boundary_test (code integer NOT NULL, character_value varchar NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO boundary_test (code, character_value) VALUES (0, chr(0));
INSERT INTO boundary_test (code, character_value) VALUES (127, chr(127));
INSERT INTO boundary_test (code, character_value) VALUES (255, chr(255));
SELECT code, character_value FROM boundary_test WHERE code BETWEEN 0 AND 255;
SELECT (SELECT chr(65) UNION SELECT chr(66) UNION SELECT chr(67)) AS characters;
SELECT t1.id, t1.char_code, chr(t1.char_code) AS character, t2.additional_info FROM test_table t1 JOIN (SELECT 1 AS id, 'Additional' AS additional_info) t2 ON t1.id = t2.id;
